heading: Process for accreditation and approval; role of accrediting entities
label: 14922 Process for accreditation and approval; role of accrediting entities
notes: References in TextThis subchapter, referred to in subsec. (a)(1), was in the original “this title”, meaning title II of , , , which is classified principally to this subchapter. For complete classification of title II to the Code, see Tables.Pub. L. 106–279114 Stat. 830Oct. 6, 2000

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall enter into agreements with one or more qualified entities under which such entities will perform the duties described in subsection (b) in accordance with the Convention, this subchapter, and the regulations prescribed under , and upon entering into each such agreement shall designate the qualified entity as an accrediting entity.section 14923 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a nonprofit private entity that has expertise in developing and administering standards for entities providing child welfare services and that meets such other criteria as the Secretary may by regulation establish; or

children.label: i
children.text: has expertise in developing and administering standards for entities providing child welfare services;

children.label: ii
children.text: accredits only agencies located in the State in which the public entity is located; and

children.label: iii
children.text: meets such other criteria as the Secretary may by regulation establish.

children.heading: Accreditation and approval
children.label: 1 Accreditation and approval
children.text: Accreditation of agencies, and approval of persons, to provide adoption services in the United States in cases subject to the Convention.

children.heading: Oversight
children.label: 2 Oversight
children.text: Ongoing monitoring of the compliance of accredited agencies and approved persons with applicable requirements, including review of complaints against such agencies and persons in accordance with procedures established by the accrediting entity and approved by the Secretary.

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: 3 Enforcement
children.text: Taking of adverse actions (including requiring corrective action, imposing sanctions, and refusing to renew, suspending, or canceling accreditation or approval) for noncompliance with applicable requirements, and notifying the agency or person against whom adverse actions are taken of the deficiencies necessitating the adverse action.

children.heading: Data, records, and reports
children.label: 4 Data, records, and reports
children.text: Collection of data, maintenance of records, and reporting to the Secretary, the United States central authority, State courts, and other entities (including on persons and agencies granted or denied approval or accreditation), to the extent and in the manner that the Secretary requires.

children.heading: Report on use of Federal funding
children.label: 5 Report on use of Federal funding
A
children.text: the amount of such funding the entity received; and

children.label: B
children.text: how such funding was, or will be, used by the entity.

children.heading: Remedies for adverse action by accrediting entity
children.label: c Remedies for adverse action by accrediting entity

children.heading: Correction of deficiency
children.label: 1 Correction of deficiency
children.text: An agency or person who is the subject of an adverse action by an accrediting entity may re-apply for accreditation or approval (or petition for termination of the adverse action) on demonstrating to the satisfaction of the accrediting entity that the deficiencies necessitating the adverse action have been corrected.

children.heading: No other administrative review
children.label: 2 No other administrative review
children.text: An adverse action by an accrediting entity shall not be subject to administrative review.

children.heading: Judicial review
children.label: 3 Judicial review
children.text: An agency or person who is the subject of an adverse action by an accrediting entity may petition the United States district court in the judicial district in which the agency is located or the person resides to set aside the adverse action. The court shall review the adverse action in accordance with , and for purposes of such review the accrediting entity shall be considered an agency within the meaning of section 701 of such title.section 706 of title 5

children.heading: Fees
children.label: d Fees
children.text: The amount of fees assessed by accrediting entities for the costs of accreditation shall be subject to approval by the Secretary. Such fees may not exceed the costs of accreditation. In reviewing the level of such fees, the Secretary shall consider the relative size of, the geographic location of, and the number of Convention adoption cases managed by the agencies or persons subject to accreditation or approval by the accrediting entity.
